deductions.description: Slight pike in hips during post-flight

deduction_log.narrative: The judge observed a slight pike in the hips during the post-flight phase of the Handspring Flat Back, indicating a momentary break in the straight body line. This form break, combined with slightly flexed feet and a small hop on landing, resulted in a total deduction of 0.25 for execution. To improve, the gymnast should focus on maintaining a tight hollow body position throughout the post-flight and actively pointing the toes through the landing. A judge would note the strong block and good height, but the minor form breaks in the air and on landing prevented a perfectly clean score.
deduction_log.injury_signal: Vaulting places significant impact loading on the ankles, knees, hips, and spine during the landing phase, as well as wrist and shoulder compression during the block. The controlled landing with a small hop suggests good absorption, but consistent monitoring of joint health, especially ankles and knees, is crucial for high-repetition vault training.
deduction_log.reason: minor form breaks and landing adjustment

coaching_summary: The athlete demonstrated a solid Handspring Flat Back vault with good power and height. The primary areas for refinement are maintaining a perfectly straight body line in the post-flight and achieving a stuck landing. Consistent attention to toe point and knee tension will elevate the execution score. From a judging standpoint, the vault was well-executed for the level, but minor form breaks were present.

artistry.notes: Minor deduction for overall body tension and presentation during the vault.

special_requirements.comment: A Handspring Flat Back was performed.

top_3_fixes: Maintain a tight hollow body position with locked knees and pointed toes throughout the entire post-flight phase to eliminate the slight hip pike.
Focus on a controlled, 'sticky' landing by driving the chest up and absorbing with bent knees, aiming to eliminate the small hop.
Actively point the toes on landing to avoid flexed feet deductions, which are easily visible to judges.
